name: market-research-reports
tagline: Generates 50+ page McKinsey-style market research reports (LaTeX/PDF) with auto-generated charts via companion skills.
verdict: No security red flags found: no credential access, no piped remote-code execution, no hidden or obfuscated instructions, no destructive commands. Main issues are hygiene, not safety: the file is ~905 lines (nearly 2x the ~500-line budget) and gives no negative-trigger guidance, so a user may over-invoke it for short reports. It also assumes three sibling ski

highlights.summary: Workflow assumes sibling skills exist at fixed relative paths (skills/scientific-schematics/..., skills/generate-image/..., skills/research-lookup/..., line 63, 81, 458) with no version pinning or int
No 'do not use when' guidance is given anywhere in the file, so a user has no signal to avoid invoking this heavy 50+-page/LaTeX workflow for a quick market summary.

At ~905 lines the skill is verbose and repeats similar per-chapter visual/required-content boilerplate 11 times (lines 128-415) instead of factoring it into the referenced references/report_structure_
